_meta.purpose: Spec 35 Phase 2 optimal-control registry — 60 CSS-property rows (category: css-property).
_meta.spec: .claude/specs/35-BLOCK-INSPECTOR-UX-STANDARD.md (Parts B / C / D / G / H / I are the optimal-control authority)

rows.label: Text colour

optimal_control.reset_disclosure: native ColorPalette Clear button; sits in the block's colour ToolsPanel group

rows.spec_ref: Part B Colour row + Part I (DesignTokenPicker exists, needs enableAlpha/clearable audit)
rows.current_state: 70 distinct attr names / 125 instances across 43 blocks; observed_control_types: DesignTokenPicker, RangeControl, SelectControl, TextControl, ToggleControl; observed_roles: color; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 70 attr names span colour AND a handful of behaviour/select uses (dedup groups every *Colour suffix together); several blocks use RangeControl/SelectControl/TextControl/ToggleControl instead of DesignTokenPicker, and DesignTokenPicker itself is not yet alpha-audited everywhere
rows.notes: Highest-instance-count setting (125) — the single biggest lever in the registry. Some observed_control_types (RangeControl/ToggleControl) are almost certainly OTHER settings that share the dedup key by accident (e.g. a boolean 'use custom colour' toggle) — worth a spot-check, flagged in the parent report.

rows.label: Grid columns
rows.spec_ref: Part H (bounded numeric -> RangeControl) + Part G (native CSS-grid layout support has a columnCount UI but no per-breakpoint tiers, which SGS needs per D2)
rows.current_state: 7 distinct attr names / 112 instances across 19 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, SelectControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 7 attrs, second-highest instance count in the whole registry (112) mixing RangeControl + SelectControl; already the closest-to-optimal shape of the whole set
rows.notes: 112 instances make this the #2 highest-impact row after color. Native WP CSS-grid layout support (WP 6.7+) covers the non-responsive case for free — worth checking whether some of these 7 attrs could drop to native support and only the genuinely-responsive ones keep the bespoke RangeControl+ResponsiveControl.

rows.label: Padding (all sides)

optimal_control.preset_layer: SpacingSizesControl token presets seed each side alongside the raw BoxControl
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset (per side + resetAll for the whole box)

rows.spec_ref: Part B BoxControl row + Part D4 (MULTI-VALUE dimension) + Part H
rows.current_state: 23 distinct attr names / 106 instances across 39 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, TextControl; observed_roles: layout, visual; box_families: contentBandPadding, contentPadding, imagePadding, mediaPadding, padding, pillPadding; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 23 attrs carry SIX different box_family names (contentBandPadding, contentPadding, imagePadding, mediaPadding, padding, pillPadding) — every one of these is a genuine 4-side padding family that should be its own BoxControl instance, but only RangeControl/TextControl are observed, meaning none of the 6 are currently link/unlink 4-side controls
rows.notes: This is the archetypal Part F 'no BoxControl, one linked number' failure, repeated 6 times under different names. Fix is universal: one BoxControl per box_family, not a padding-specific special case (dimension 4).

rows.label: Font size

optimal_control.preset_layer: FontSizePicker presets seed the box (custom value always editable)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset; lives in the Typography ToolsPanel

rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography + Part H (font size -> FontSizePicker/UnitControl)
rows.current_state: 62 distinct attr names / 105 instances across 21 blocks; observed_control_types: TextControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: blocks on a bare RangeControl or px-only lack the unit set + preset seeding
rows.notes: Worked example from the design spine, reused verbatim. Per-element (title/label/price), not wrapper — TypographyControls owns the shape.

rows.label: Width
rows.spec_ref: Part B UnitControl row + Part H (numeric+unit -> UnitControl) + Part I (ResponsiveControl exists)
rows.current_state: 24 distinct attr names / 91 instances across 56 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, ResponsiveControl, SelectControl, ToggleControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: review)
rows.divergence: 24 attrs mix RangeControl/SelectControl/ToggleControl/UnitControl — only the UnitControl instances meet the bar; SelectControl usages read as a small fixed preset list (e.g. 'full/half/third') rather than a real editable box
rows.notes: Already has some ResponsiveControl coverage (best-conforming of the mixed group) — the fix is consolidating the RangeControl/SelectControl stragglers onto the same UnitControl+ResponsiveControl shape, not building something new.

rows.label: Margin (all sides)

optimal_control.preset_layer: SpacingSizesControl token presets seed each side

rows.spec_ref: Part B BoxControl row + Part D4 + Part H
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 80 instances across 41 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: layout; box_families: margin; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 4 attrs, box_family 'margin' correctly identified, but no observed_control_types at all (conformance: unknown) — cannot confirm any block currently exposes a margin control at all rather than a hardcoded render.php value
rows.notes: Needs a live-code check: if margin is currently ONLY set via CSS/render.php with no editor control, this is a straight MISSING build, not a divergence fix.

rows.label: Gap (row/column spacing)

optimal_control.component: UnitControl inside ResponsiveControl (native layout.spacing.blockGap where the block genuinely never needs per-tier gap)
optimal_control.preset_layer: SpacingSizesControl token presets seed the box (theme.json spacing scale) alongside the raw UnitControl

rows.spec_ref: Part B Spacing row (token-based SpacingSizesControl) + Part G (native layout.spacing.blockGap, HIGH) + Part H
rows.current_state: 9 distinct attr names / 65 instances across 25 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, SelectControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 9 attrs mix RangeControl/SelectControl/UnitControl; none currently offer the token-preset layer (dimension 1) on top of the raw box
rows.notes: gap is the highest-value candidate for adopting the theme.json spacing token scale (SpacingSizesControl) rather than raw pixels — most card-grid/flex composite blocks want '40/Medium' not '38px'.

rows.label: Max width

optimal_control.units: px/%/rem/vw; empty = full-width (theme default), never a hardcoded fallback per R-31-9
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset — clearing MUST mean 'no max-width', not silently re-apply a default

rows.spec_ref: Part B UnitControl row + CLAUDE.md 'a property's absence -> full-width, overriding the theme default'
rows.current_state: 7 distinct attr names / 58 instances across 38 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, SelectControl, TextControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 7 attrs mix RangeControl/SelectControl/TextControl/UnitControl; TextControl is a free-text field for what should be a validated unit value
rows.notes: This is also the container/wrapper contentWidth capability (Spec 31 §13.6 3-layer model) — must not silently reintroduce a hardcoded default per the 'hardcoded wrapper defaults are cheats' rule in CLAUDE.md.

rows.label: Grid rows

optimal_control.component: RangeControl (row count) inside ResponsiveControl, + a Custom text path for an explicit track-list string
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: count-based like grid-template-columns, with a Custom escape hatch since some grids need an explicit track list (e.g. minmax()), not just a count

rows.spec_ref: Part H + mirrors grid-template-columns treatment
rows.current_state: 3 distinct attr names / 51 instances across 17 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: none; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 3 attrs, 51 instances, but NO observed control at all (conformance: unknown) — cannot confirm a client-facing row-count control exists anywhere today
rows.notes: 51 instances with zero observed controls is the strongest 'likely missing entirely' signal in the whole 60-row set outside the explicit Part I MISSING items. Needs a live-code audit before committing to build scope.

rows.label: Background colour
rows.spec_ref: Part B Colour row + Part I
rows.current_state: 37 distinct attr names / 49 instances across 25 blocks; observed_control_types: DesignTokenPicker, RangeControl, SelectControl, ToggleControl; observed_roles: color; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 37 attr names, 49 instances; SelectControl/ToggleControl/RangeControl usages likely mean some blocks only offer a preset SWATCH list or an on/off 'tinted background' toggle rather than a full picker — both are incomplete per Part F ('incomplete option sets').

rows.label: Border radius

optimal_control.preset_layer: theme.json custom border-radius tokens seed the corner control where available

rows.spec_ref: Part B BoxControl-family row + Part H (radius -> __experimentalBorderRadiusControl)
rows.current_state: 16 distinct attr names / 37 instances across 16 blocks; observed_control_types: DesignTokenPicker, RangeControl, TextControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: typography, visual; box_families: borderRadius, imageBorderRadius; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 16 attrs across 2 box_families (borderRadius, imageBorderRadius) on DesignTokenPicker/RangeControl/TextControl/UnitControl — none are the dedicated 4-corner control; several look like single-value 'rounded corners' toggles
rows.notes: Same multi-value failure as padding, at the corner level: link/unlink 4-corner control, not a single scalar. Marked non-responsive by default since radius rarely needs a device tier, but flag if a specific block proves otherwise.

rows.label: Line height
rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography row + Part H (line-height -> LineHeightControl/UnitControl)
rows.current_state: 17 distinct attr names / 31 instances across 13 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl, TextControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: TypographyControls already implements the correct unitless-capable UnitControl (17 attrs, 31 instances) but SGS_TEXT_ line-height carries NO Tablet/Mobile companions the way fontSize does — line-height cannot currently be tiered even though it is squarely a typography setting under dimension 3
rows.notes: Optimal adds lineHeightTablet/lineHeightMobile mirroring the fontSize pattern in TypographyControls.js. Some observed SelectControl usages are pre-TypographyControls legacy and should migrate.

rows.label: Transition duration
rows.spec_ref: Part C Motion (prefers-reduced-motion gating, T) — this control feeds a reduced-motion-gated transition, never itself
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 31 instances across 22 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, SelectControl, TextControl; observed_roles: motion; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 4 attrs mix RangeControl/SelectControl/TextControl — none expose a real ms/s unit pair; RangeControl instances look 0-100 with no unit label (Part B RangeControl FAIL smell)
rows.notes: Gate at render: whatever duration is set here must be wrapped by a `prefers-reduced-motion` media query per Part C/E5 so the control's value never overrides the reduced-motion contract.

rows.label: Transition easing

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (linear / ease / ease-in / ease-out / ease-in-out) + Custom cubic-bezier text path
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 5 short named options + an explicit Custom cubic-bezier(...) path — meets dimension 5's 'never a truncated set' bar since raw CSS timing functions are open-ended

rows.spec_ref: Part C Motion + Part H (segmented choice -> ToggleGroupControl)
rows.current_state: 3 distinct attr names / 26 instances across 19 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, SelectControl; observed_roles: motion; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 3 attrs on RangeControl/SelectControl; RangeControl for a named-keyword property is a clear Part B mismatch (numeric slider driving a CSS keyword)
rows.notes: Same reduced-motion gating note as transition-duration applies here.

rows.label: Justify content

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (flex-start / center / flex-end / space-between / space-around / space-evenly) inside ResponsiveControl
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 6 short icon-labelled options (native WP flex layout justifyContent only covers left/center/right/space-between — SGS needs the fuller CSS set plus device tiers, which native support doesn't provide)

rows.spec_ref: Part H + Part G (native layout support is the PARTIAL alternative, not a full replacement here)
rows.current_state: 3 distinct attr names / 23 instances across 21 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: none; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 3 attrs, already consistent on SelectControl. Real gap is adding the responsive tier (mobile often wants centered where desktop wants space-between) and the full 6-value CSS set.

rows.label: Minimum height

optimal_control.component: UnitControl inside ResponsiveControl (native dimensions.minHeight support where the block doesn't need per-tier values)

rows.spec_ref: Part B UnitControl row + Part G ('Block supports: dimensions.minHeight' HIGH priority — prefer native over bespoke panel)
rows.current_state: 7 distinct attr names / 22 instances across 5 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: layout; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: no observed_control_types at all (conformance: unknown) across 7 attrs/22 instances — cannot confirm any of these blocks expose an editable min-height control today
rows.notes: Genuine unknown: either (a) it's set once via native `supports.dimensions.minHeight` and never surfaced as a distinct named React control (in which case it may already be fine and just invisible to this audit), or (b) it's a converter-only/render.php-only value with no client control at all. Needs a live-code check per block before treating as a build gap — flagged for parent.

rows.label: Font weight

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: SelectControl (10 options incl. inherit) — right at the Combobox threshold but kept as SelectControl to match the already-built, audited shared component; a ToggleGroupControl would be too wide for 10 items

rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography row + Part H
rows.current_state: 11 distinct attr names / 21 instances across 16 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 11 attrs, already consistent conformance. The only real gap is the handful of attrs not yet routed through TypographyControls at all.

rows.label: Display type

optimal_control.reset_disclosure: clears to the block's native default display

rows.spec_ref: Part H (segmented choice -> ToggleGroupControl) + Part D2 (this is exactly the 'block on mobile, grid on desktop' pattern D2 exists for)
rows.current_state: 2 distinct attr names / 21 instances across 20 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 2 attrs, already consistent on SelectControl — swap component only; the responsive-tier gap (a device switch from grid->block on mobile) is the bigger real-world win here.

rows.label: Flex wrap

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 3-option closed CSS enum (native WP flex layout support has a plain wrap on/off toggle but not the reverse variant)

rows.spec_ref: Part H + Part G
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 20 instances across 20 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: none; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 1 attr, already consistent on SelectControl — component swap + add the responsive tier, since wrapping behaviour commonly needs to differ by device.

rows.label: Box shadow

optimal_control.preset_layer: theme.json shadow presets seed the builder; every field stays editable

rows.spec_ref: Part B Shadow row (real builder, FAIL smell = None/Small/Medium select) + Part I ('MISSING — build shared ShadowControl') + Part J Wave 1 item 3
rows.current_state: 6 distinct attr names / 19 instances across 10 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, SelectControl, TextControl; observed_roles: behaviour, color; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: review)
rows.divergence: 6 attrs on RangeControl/SelectControl/TextControl — textbook Part B FAIL smell: a None/Small/Medium-style select with no real X/Y/blur/spread/colour builder
rows.notes: This is Wave-1 Part J item 3 — ShadowControl is listed as 'built this phase' in the blessed-components table, so the fix is rollout (swap every block's ad hoc shadow select for ShadowControl), not a new build.

rows.label: Height
rows.spec_ref: Part B UnitControl row + Part H
rows.current_state: 11 distinct attr names / 19 instances across 6 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, TextControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 11 attrs mix RangeControl/TextControl/UnitControl
rows.notes: Same shape as width — consolidate onto UnitControl+ResponsiveControl.

rows.label: Align content

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (flex-start / flex-end / center / space-between / space-around / stretch) inside ResponsiveControl

rows.spec_ref: Part H
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 17 instances across 17 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: none; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 1 attr, no observed control at all across 17 instances (conformance: unknown) — same 'strong missing signal' pattern as flex-direction/grid-auto-rows; needs a live-code check.

rows.label: Align items

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (flex-start / center / flex-end / stretch / baseline) inside ResponsiveControl

rows.spec_ref: Part H + Part G (native layout support covers verticalAlignment for flex partially)
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 17 instances across 15 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 4 attrs, already consistent on SelectControl.

rows.label: Flex direction

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (row / column / row-reverse / column-reverse) inside ResponsiveControl
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 4-option closed CSS enum (native WP flex layout support only exposes horizontal/vertical, not the reverse variants, so SGS needs its own control for full parity)

rows.spec_ref: Part H + Part C ('responsive column ordering' P) + Part G (native layout support covers the simple case, not reverse)
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 17 instances across 17 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: none; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 1 attr, no observed control at all (conformance: unknown) across 17 instances — cannot confirm any client-facing control exists for this property today
rows.notes: 17 instances with zero observed controls is a strong MISSING signal, not just a wrong-component one. If it turns out to be render.php/CSS-only, that's a real client-facing gap (stack order on mobile is a very common ask) per Part C 'responsive column ordering'.

rows.label: Grid auto row height

optimal_control.component: UnitControl (px / fr / rem / auto) inside ResponsiveControl

rows.spec_ref: Part H (numeric+unit -> UnitControl)
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 17 instances across 17 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: none; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 1 attr, no observed control at all across 17 instances (conformance: unknown)
rows.notes: Same missing-signal pattern as align-content/flex-direction/grid-template-rows — all four share 17 or 51 instances with zero observed controls. Worth a single combined live-code audit rather than four separate ones, since they may all trace to the same composite grid block.

rows.label: Justify items (grid)
rows.spec_ref: Part H
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 17 instances across 17 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: none; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 1 attr, already consistent on SelectControl — component swap + responsive tier only.

rows.label: Font style

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 3 short options — a ToggleGroupControl would be more idiomatic per dimension 5, but this is the already-built shared component; not worth diverging from the blessed component for 3 items

rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography row
rows.current_state: 7 distinct attr names / 16 instances across 15 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 7 attrs, consistent conformance already.

rows.label: Opacity
rows.spec_ref: Part H (bounded numeric -> RangeControl) + Part C (opacity T)
rows.current_state: 6 distinct attr names / 15 instances across 9 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl; observed_roles: visual; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 6 attrs, already consistent on RangeControl — the textbook already-optimal case in the registry. Only worth checking each instance genuinely has withInputField + allowReset (Part B RangeControl completeness bar), not just a bare slider.

rows.label: Border colour

optimal_control.component: DesignTokenPicker (as the colour slot inside BorderBoxControl)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: native ColorPalette Clear button, shared reset with the border builder

rows.spec_ref: Part B Border row + Part I
rows.current_state: 9 distinct attr names / 15 instances across 12 blocks; observed_control_types: DesignTokenPicker, SelectControl; observed_roles: color; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 9 attr names; mix of DesignTokenPicker + SelectControl. Should be one slot inside a unified border builder alongside border-width/border-style/border-radius, not a standalone picker per block.

rows.label: Border width
rows.spec_ref: Part B Border row (BorderBoxControl: enableStyle + enableAlpha + per-side split) + Part D4
rows.current_state: 5 distinct attr names / 12 instances across 12 blocks; observed_control_types: DesignTokenPicker, SelectControl; observed_roles: visual; box_families: borderWidth, imageBorderWidth; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 5 attrs across 2 box_families (borderWidth, imageBorderWidth) on DesignTokenPicker/SelectControl — neither is a per-side border-width control; both read as single-value pickers
rows.notes: Should unify with border-color + border-style + border-radius into ONE BorderBoxControl builder per box_family, not four separate settings each with their own half-built control.

rows.label: Letter spacing
rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography row
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 12 instances across 4 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl, ResponsiveControl, SelectControl, UnitControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: TypographyControls' showLetterSpacing path is correct where used, but 4 attrs mix RangeControl/ResponsiveControl/SelectControl/UnitControl directly rather than the shared component, and none currently expose Tablet/Mobile tiers
rows.notes: Same tiering gap as line-height — add letterSpacingTablet/Mobile to the shared component rather than per-block.

rows.label: Border style

optimal_control.component: Segmented control inside the same BorderBoxControl as border-width/colour
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 5-option closed CSS enum — folds into BorderBoxControl's native style picker rather than a standalone ToggleGroupControl

rows.spec_ref: Part B Border row
rows.current_state: 3 distinct attr names / 10 instances across 10 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: visual; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 3 attrs, already consistent on SelectControl — functionally fine today; the only real gap is that it's a separate control from width/colour/radius instead of one unified builder.

rows.label: Margin — bottom

optimal_control.component: NOT a standalone control — folds into the css:margin BoxControl as one side
optimal_control.units: n/a (inherits px/rem/% from the parent BoxControl)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: resets via the parent BoxControl's per-side or resetAll

rows.spec_ref: Part B/D4 MULTI-VALUE
rows.current_state: 8 distinct attr names / 10 instances across 5 blocks; observed_control_types: TextControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 8 attrs on TextControl (consistent conformance) — a working scalar field, but still one-at-a-time rather than the shared 4-side BoxControl
rows.notes: Drafted per the schema's one-row-per-property rule; optimal control is the shared margin BoxControl. margin-bottom is the only one of the four with consistent conformance/TextControl usage already — still a per-side scalar to fold in.

rows.label: Text alignment

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 4 options via native icon toggle — closed CSS enum, no Custom path needed
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: native 'reset to default' behaviour of AlignmentControl

rows.spec_ref: Part H (native components) + Part G (prefer native, don't hand-roll)
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 9 instances across 5 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: none; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 4 attrs all on bespoke SelectControl rather than WP's own AlignmentControl/AlignmentToolbar, and none are responsive-tiered even though 'centre on mobile, left on desktop' is a very common real request
rows.notes: This is a Part G 'prefer native' catch: WordPress ships AlignmentControl purpose-built for exactly this. Also a genuine responsive-tier gap (dimension 3) since text-align commonly needs to flip per breakpoint.

rows.label: Margin — top

optimal_control.component: NOT a standalone control — folds into the css:margin BoxControl as one side
optimal_control.units: n/a (inherits px/rem/% from the parent BoxControl)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: resets via the parent BoxControl's per-side or resetAll

rows.spec_ref: Part B/D4 MULTI-VALUE
rows.current_state: 5 distinct attr names / 7 instances across 3 blocks; observed_control_types: none captured; observed_roles: layout; conformance: unknown (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: Drafted per the schema's one-row-per-property rule; optimal control is the shared margin BoxControl. margin-bottom is the only one of the four with consistent conformance/TextControl usage already — still a per-side scalar to fold in.

rows.label: Font family

optimal_control.component: ComboboxControl (searchable — theme.json font families + Font Library collection)
optimal_control.preset_layer: theme.json families act as the curated preset list; Font Library modal is the 'more fonts' escape hatch
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ComboboxControl — a searchable list of potentially hundreds of fonts is exactly the >~10 case Part H names for Combobox, not a plain SelectControl

rows.spec_ref: Part H (long/searchable list -> ComboboxControl) + Font Library Collection (plugins/sgs-blocks CLAUDE.md Backend Integrations)
rows.current_state: 2 distinct attr names / 5 instances across 5 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl, TextControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 2 attrs use SelectControl/TextControl today — a plain SelectControl can't scale to the Font Library's font count without becoming an unusable giant dropdown
rows.notes: Low instance count (5) but real gap: SelectControl doesn't scale once a client installs extra fonts via the Font Library. No shared SGS component for this yet — flagged as a build gap in the report.

rows.label: Text decoration

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 5 short options incl. inherit — borderline for ToggleGroupControl per dimension 5, kept as the existing shared-component SelectControl

rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography row
rows.current_state: 2 distinct attr names / 5 instances across 5 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl, TextControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 2 attrs mix SelectControl + TextControl — the TextControl path is a free-text field for what should be a closed CSS enum
rows.notes: The TextControl instance is the actual gap here (open-text field for a closed enum = Part F anti-pattern), not the SelectControl one.

rows.label: Text transform

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: 5 short options; matches the shared-component pattern

rows.spec_ref: Part B Typography row
rows.current_state: 2 distinct attr names / 5 instances across 5 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: typography; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 2 attrs, consistent conformance already.

rows.label: Maximum height
rows.spec_ref: Part B UnitControl row
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 5 instances across 2 blocks; observed_control_types: RangeControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.notes: 4 attrs, already consistent on RangeControl — the gap is only that RangeControl lacks a real unit selector (px-only smell per Part B), not the responsive shape.

rows.label: Flex/grid item order

optimal_control.component: RangeControl (bounded, e.g. -5 to 5, withInputField+allowReset) inside ResponsiveControl

rows.spec_ref: Part H (bounded numeric -> RangeControl) + Part C ('responsive column ordering' P)
rows.current_state: 4 distinct attr names / 5 instances across 3 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl; observed_roles: none; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: 4 attrs, already consistent on SelectControl — a fixed dropdown of order values works but caps the range arbitrarily and has no per-breakpoint reorder, which is the actual client value here
rows.notes: The real product here is Part C's 'responsive column ordering' — reordering a card on mobile without moving it in the DOM. Component swap alone (Select->Range) is secondary to adding the responsive tier.

rows.label: Aspect ratio

optimal_control.component: ToggleGroupControl (1:1, 4:3, 16:9, 21:9) + Custom text entry — mirrors native dimensions.aspectRatio support UI
optimal_control.preset_layer: the ratio ToggleGroupControl IS the preset layer; 'Custom' reveals a free-text ratio field
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl (short closed set) + explicit Custom path — meets dimension 5 exactly

rows.spec_ref: Part C Media (native dimensions.aspectRatio) + Part G (HIGH — prefer native over hand-rolled)
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 4 instances across 4 blocks; observed_control_types: SelectControl, TextControl; observed_roles: layout; conformance: divergent (dedup_confidence: high)
rows.divergence: the 1 observed instance uses SelectControl/TextControl rather than WP's native aspectRatio support, which already ships this exact UI for free
rows.notes: Part G HIGH-priority native-adoption item — SGS should declare `supports.dimensions.aspectRatio: true` rather than hand-rolling. Native support isn't per-breakpoint though, so a block needing a genuinely different ratio on mobile (e.g. square avatar image widening to 16:9 hero) still needs the bespoke ResponsiveControl-wrapped version; note both paths.

FocalPointPicker is visually the right shape but semantically positions a foreground image inside its own box, not a background layer; a genuinely new small SGS component (`BackgroundPositionControl`) may be the cleanest fix rather than repurposing FocalPointPicker.

rows.label: Icon stroke colour
rows.spec_ref: Part B Colour row (stroke is a paint property, same completeness bar as color/border-color)
rows.current_state: 1 distinct attr names / 1 instances across 1 blocks; observed_control_types: ToggleControl; observed_roles: behaviour; conformance: consistent (dedup_confidence: review)
rows.divergence: the CSS property is a colour/paint value but the only observed control is a boolean ToggleControl — reads as an on/off 'coloured stroke?' switch, not an actual colour picker
rows.notes: UNCERTAIN — only 1 instance, role tagged 'behaviour' not 'color'. Two readings: (a) it genuinely sets stroke paint and the current ToggleControl is a broken/incomplete implementation (my assumption above), or (b) it is really a boolean 'use stroke variant of icon' switch dedup'd onto the stroke property by name-matching and belongs in the behaviour-family registry, not here. Flagging for parent — see report.
